description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, review, demo, and production and define variables for APP_IMAGE, DEMO_APP, DEMO_APP_DB, DEMO_APP_DOMAIN, DEMO_NET, REVIEW_APP, REVIEW_APP_DOMAIN, SERVER_DOMAIN, and SLACK_URL. In the build stage add a build job that runs the before_script commands `git submodule sync` and `git submodule update --init -- SpicyCMS_Chief_Editor`, then executes `echo "image name is $APP_IMAGE"` and `docker build --tag $APP_IMAGE .`. In the demo stage add a demo job that runs only on the master branch, checks and creates the Docker network `$DEMO_NET` if missing, stops and removes any existing containers `$DEMO_APP` and `$DEMO_APP_DB`, starts a Postgres container named `$DEMO_APP_DB` with `docker run --detach --name $DEMO_APP_DB --network $DEMO_NET --env POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres postgres`, then starts the application container `$DEMO_APP` with a long list of `--env` flags using `$APP_IMAGE`, connects it to `$DEMO_NET` with `docker network connect $DEMO_NET $DEMO_APP`, echoes the URL, and sends a Slack notification via `curl -X POST -d "$SLACK_PAYLOAD" $SLACK_URL`. In the review stage add a review job that stops and removes any existing `$REVIEW_APP` container, runs a new container `$REVIEW_APP` with SQLite settings using `$APP_IMAGE`, echoes its URL, builds a Slack message that includes the latest git commit body, and posts it with `curl -X POST -d "$SLACK_PAYLOAD" $SLACK_URL`. No caches, artifacts, or additional services are defined.
